proposed_hypotheses.text: At least some of the 123 features in ds001_crc are individually associated with pfs_months. A univariate screen will identify a small set of candidate predictors with p < 1e-6 (signed direction to be confirmed).

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
for each continuous feature: spearmanr(feature, pfs_months); for each binary: Welch t-test; for each multi-level: one-way ANOVA
analyses.result_summary: A small set of features show extremely strong univariate association with pfs_months (p effectively 0): feature_078 (Spearman r=+0.785), feature_051 (binary, mean diff -1.35 months for 1 vs 0), feature_057 (3-level, max-min diff +2.36 months), feature_038 (binary, +0.97 months for 1), feature_099 (Spearman r=-0.12, slope -0.073/unit), feature_092 (slope +0.10/unit), feature_013 (-0.33 months), feature_043 (-0.31 months), feature_109 (-0.23 months), feature_009 (slope -0.005/unit). 19 of 123 features reach p<0.05; the rest are non-significant univariately.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65, granularity of 0.1) is positively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_078 values predict longer pfs_months in a monotonic dose-response.
feature_057 (a 3-level ordinal feature with values 0/1/2) shows monotonic decrease in pfs_months as the level increases, consistent with a stage- or grade-like prognostic variable.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.formula.api as smf
m = smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078', data=df).fit()
# also: quintile means
analyses.result_summary: Linear regression slope = +0.176 months per unit feature_078 (p<<1e-300). Quintile means of pfs_months: Q1 [30.0-56.5]=1.86, Q2 [56.6-62.4]=3.34, Q3 [62.5-67.6]=4.30, Q4 [67.7-73.5]=5.27, Q5 [73.6-90.0]=6.83 — strict monotonic dose-response. feature_078 is the single strongest predictor in the dataset; range from min to max corresponds to ~10.6-month difference in mean pfs_months.

analyses.code: for v in [0,1,2]: df.loc[df['feature_057']==v,'pfs_months'].mean(); one-way ANOVA
analyses.result_summary: Strict monotonic decrease in pfs_months across feature_057 levels: level 0 mean 5.27 (n=17592), level 1 mean 4.05 (n=24971), level 2 mean 2.91 (n=7437). One-way ANOVA p<<1e-300. Adjusted-model coefficients confirm: level 1 vs 0 = -1.20 months (p<1e-300); level 2 vs 0 = -2.33 months (p<1e-300). Pattern and prevalence are consistent with a stage-like ordinal prognostic variable (e.g., AJCC stage groups).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (binary, prevalence 0.55) is associated with shorter pfs_months: patients with feature_051=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with feature_051=0, independent of feature_078 and feature_057.

feature_038 (binary, prevalence 0.20) is associated with longer pfs_months: patients with feature_038=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with feature_038=0, plausibly representing a positive prognostic biomarker.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_051=1 vs 0: unadjusted mean pfs_months 3.70 vs 5.06 (Welch t-test p<<1e-300, diff -1.35 months). Adjusted for feature_078 and feature_057, feature_051 coefficient = -1.36 months (p<1e-300). Effect is robust within every stratum of feature_057 (level 0: -1.39; level 1: -1.34; level 2: -1.31; all p<<1e-100), so feature_051 acts approximately additively on the pfs_months scale across stages.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_038', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_038=1 vs 0: unadjusted mean pfs_months 5.09 vs 4.12 (diff +0.97 months, p<<1e-216). Adjusted for feature_078, feature_057, and feature_051, feature_038 coefficient = +0.94 months (p<1e-300). Effect is highly significant in every stage stratum (level 0: +0.99; level 1: +0.92; level 2: +0.88).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (binary, prevalence 0.42) is associated with shorter pfs_months independent of stage and feature_078.
feature_043 (binary, prevalence 0.35) is associated with shorter pfs_months independent of stage and feature_078.
feature_109 (rare binary, prevalence 0.045) is associated with shorter pfs_months independent of other predictors.

feature_067 (rare binary, prevalence 0.030), which appeared as a positive univariate signal, will lose statistical significance after adjusting for feature_078 and feature_057 — i.e. the univariate effect is confounded.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_109 + feature_067', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In the adjusted multivariable model: feature_013 coef = -0.353 months (p<1e-300, supports h6); feature_043 coef = -0.287 months (p<1e-300, supports h7); feature_109 coef = -0.357 months (p=2e-78, supports h8); feature_067 coef = +0.021 months (p=0.37, fails to reject null — effect is fully confounded by other predictors, supporting h9 that the univariate +0.22-month feature_067 signal does not survive adjustment).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_099 (continuous, range 0-24.6, with 27.5% of values exactly 0) is monotonically negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other predictors.
feature_092 (continuous, narrow range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.80) is positively associated with pfs_months and the relationship is approximately linear (no major curvature).

feature_009 (continuous, heavy right-skew, range 0.02-777) is negatively associated with pfs_months — higher values predict shorter pfs_months — consistent with a tumor-burden or biomarker-burden lab.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_099', data=df).fit(); also rank-quintile means
analyses.result_summary: Quintile means of pfs_months by feature_099: Q1 (0)=4.62, Q2 (0-1.7)=4.53, Q3 (1.7-4.2)=4.36, Q4 (4.2-7.2)=4.18, Q5 (7.2-24.6)=3.87 — monotonic decline. Adjusted slope = -0.076 months per unit feature_099 (p<1e-300). Supports h10.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_092 + I(feature_092**2)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear coefficient on feature_092 = +0.59 (p=3e-13); quadratic term coef = -0.016 (p=0.15, not significant). Without the quadratic term, the linear adjusted slope = +0.47/unit (p<1e-300). Supports h11: relationship is essentially linear and positive on the additive scale.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted slope on feature_009 = -0.0050 months per unit (p=4e-98 in adjusted model). Across the IQR (1.96 to 10.07) this corresponds to ~0.04-month difference; across the full range (0.02 to 777.4) up to ~3.9 months. Supports h12 in direction; effect per-unit is small but the wide range gives material variation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across racial groups represented in feature_064 (white, hispanic, black, asian, other), with non-white groups having shorter pfs_months than white patients.

Mean pfs_months differs across insurance categories represented in feature_018 (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured), with medicaid and uninsured patients having shorter pfs_months than medicare/private.

Even if there is no main-effect disparity, race or insurance interacts with stage (feature_057) such that the most disadvantaged subgroups (e.g. uninsured at high stage, black at high stage) have differentially shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*groups by feature_064); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ... + C(feature_064)')
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted means by race: white 4.316, hispanic 4.315, black 4.273, asian 4.306, other 4.407 (one-way ANOVA F=0.87, p=0.48). After adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, feature_092, feature_099, no race level differs from reference at p<0.10 (largest |coef|=0.031, all p>=0.36). Hypothesis h13 is REFUTED.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*groups by feature_018); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ... + C(feature_018)')
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted means by insurance: medicare 4.312, private 4.330, medicaid 4.277, uninsured 4.249 (one-way ANOVA F=1.48, p=0.22). After adjustment, no insurance level differs from reference (max |coef|=0.032, p=0.16). Hypothesis h14 is REFUTED.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_018)*C(feature_057) + ... ', data=df).fit(); same for race
analyses.result_summary: All race x feature_078 interaction terms p>=0.19 (largest coef -0.003/year). All insurance x feature_057 interaction terms p>=0.06 (largest coef -0.077, private:stage_2). Joint F-test for race+insurance contribution to the adjusted model: F=0.52 on 7 df, p=0.82, delta R^2=0.00001. Distribution of feature_038 (positive biomarker) is balanced across race (0.192-0.211) and insurance (0.190-0.202); feature_051 prevalence is also balanced (0.54-0.56 across all subgroups). Hypothesis h15 is REFUTED. The cohort shows no detectable racial or insurance disparity in pfs_months either as a main effect or in stage- or age-stratified interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable linear regression of pfs_months on feature_078, feature_057 (categorical), feature_051, feature_038, feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, feature_099, feature_092, and feature_009 explains a large share of the variance in pfs_months (R^2 > 0.5).

Adding additional weak univariate signals (feature_028, feature_070) to the multivariable model does not materially improve fit — i.e. these weak univariate effects are confounded by the strong predictors.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_109 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: R^2 = 0.8584 (adj R^2 = 0.8584). All ten predictors significant at p<1e-78. Coefficients: feature_078 +0.176/unit; feature_057 level 1 -1.196, level 2 -2.329; feature_051 -1.361; feature_038 +0.943; feature_013 -0.353; feature_043 -0.287; feature_109 -0.357; feature_099 -0.076/unit; feature_092 +0.470/unit; feature_009 -0.005/unit. Hypothesis h16 STRONGLY supported — variance explained far exceeds the 0.5 threshold.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ... + feature_028 + feature_070', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After adding feature_028 and feature_070 to the 10-predictor model, R^2 changes from 0.85840 to 0.85840 (delta < 1e-5). feature_028 coef = -0.005 (p=0.68); feature_070 coef = +2.4e-05 (p=0.76). Both lose their univariate significance entirely after adjustment — supports h17.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_038 (positive biomarker) interacts with feature_057 (stage): the absolute pfs_months benefit of feature_038=1 attenuates as stage increases.
feature_051 and feature_038 have independent additive effects (no statistical interaction) — i.e. the negative impact of feature_051 is not modified by feature_038 status.

feature_078 (the dominant predictor) interacts with feature_057 (stage): the positive effect of feature_078 on pfs_months is smaller at higher stages — consistent with severity dampening any prognostic gain.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ... + C(feature_057)*feature_038', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms: feature_057[T.1]:feature_038 coef = -0.060 (p=0.0050); feature_057[T.2]:feature_038 coef = -0.100 (p=0.00084). Stratified effect of feature_038 by stage: level 0 +0.985, level 1 +0.918, level 2 +0.875. The biomarker benefit is real in every stratum but is modestly smaller at higher stages, supporting h18.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ... + feature_051*feature_038', data=df).fit(); cell-mean grid
analyses.result_summary: Cell means (pfs_months): (feature_051=0, feature_038=0) 4.87; (0,1) 5.81; (1,0) 3.50; (1,1) 4.51. Implied differences: feature_038 effect at feature_051=0 is +0.94; at feature_051=1 is +1.01 — nearly identical. Interaction coef in adjusted model = +0.027, p=0.19. Hypothesis h19 supported: feature_051 and feature_038 contribute additively with no statistically significant interaction.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078*C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_038', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms: feature_078:feature_057[T.1] coef = -0.0033/year (p=5.7e-04); feature_078:feature_057[T.2] coef = -0.0170/year (p=3.8e-37). The per-unit positive feature_078 slope is reduced by ~10% at stage 2 vs stage 0. Supports h20: prognostic benefit of feature_078 is dampened at higher stages.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (binary, 42% prevalence) interacts with feature_038 (the positive biomarker): the impact of feature_013 differs by feature_038 status, suggesting feature_013 may be a treatment-like or co-condition variable whose effect depends on biomarker status.

Among biomarker-positive patients (feature_038=1), feature_013=1 is associated with substantially shorter pfs_months than feature_013=0, while in biomarker-negative patients (feature_038=0) feature_013 has essentially no association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: for v in [0,1]: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013', data=df[df['feature_038']==v]).fit(); also full model with C(feature_038)*feature_013
analyses.result_summary: STRATIFIED RESULTS: in feature_038=0 (n=39978), feature_013 coef = -0.001 (p=0.95) — essentially null. In feature_038=1 (n=10022), feature_013 coef = -1.630 (p=2.5e-182) — very large negative effect. The contrast is a textbook qualitative effect-modification pattern. Hypotheses h21 and h22 strongly supported. Pattern is consistent with feature_013 being a treatment or co-factor whose harmful association with pfs_months is restricted to biomarker-positive patients (e.g., a therapy contraindicated when feature_038 is positive, or a co-morbidity that interacts with biomarker biology).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Synthesis: a parsimonious 10-feature additive model with the feature_013 x feature_038 interaction included captures essentially all of the systematic variance in pfs_months for which any individual feature contributes >0.05 R^2.

Across this cohort, demographic features (race feature_064 and insurance feature_018) contribute no detectable variance in pfs_months once clinical predictors are accounted for — a robust null finding for outcome disparities.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_038*feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_109 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final 10-feature additive model has R^2 = 0.8584. Adding the feature_038 x feature_013 interaction further increases R^2 by ~0.005 with the interaction coef extremely significant (p<1e-100). Adding feature_028 and feature_070 changes R^2 by <1e-5. Adding race+insurance changes R^2 by 1e-5. Conclusion: a 10-12 term clinical model captures almost all explainable signal, and demographic adjustments are unnecessary on the pfs_months scale.

analyses.code: F-test comparing the 10-feature clinical model to the same model + C(feature_064) + C(feature_018)
analyses.result_summary: Joint F-test for race + insurance after clinical adjustment: F=0.520 on 7 df, p=0.820. delta R^2 = 0.00001. Stratified analyses show stable pfs_months across all race x age and insurance x age cells (e.g., young-uninsured 2.39 vs young-private 2.42; old-uninsured 6.21 vs old-private 6.24). Distribution of biomarkers (feature_038, feature_051) is essentially uniform across race and insurance. Hypothesis h24 strongly supported: no detectable racial or insurance disparity in pfs_months in this cohort.
